description: §6.2 Shape-4 const-derived carve-out (codes): a DERIVED cell with a type annotation and no RHS (`const <doubled>: int`) is a derived-with-no-expression error — E-DECL-NEEDS-INITIALIZER. Shape 4's no-RHS canonical-empty default (int→0) applies to PLAIN reactive cells only; a derived cell requires an expression. Asserted AS an error. Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 / E-DG-002 ignored by superset matching. NOTE: this case uses a NON-array type deliberately — impl#1 exempts the array form (`const <x>: T[]` historically synthesizes `[]` and stays silent), which is why the contract is pinned on the scalar-typed form.
rationale: §6.2 Shape 4, closing parenthetical (SPEC.md:2213): "A `const`-derived cell still requires an expression — `const <x>: T` with no RHS is a derived-with-no-expression error, NOT covered by this shape." The Shape-4 table (int→0, string→"", bool→false, T[]→[]) governs plain reactive cells; a derived cell's value IS its expression, so omitting the RHS leaves nothing to recompute from.
